characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: City Mouse: She grew up in a posh Boston neighborhood and has a hard time adjusting to the life in a frontier town. Daddy's Girl: She was beloved by her father, who wanted a son to follow his footsteps and so encouraged her to become a doctor like him. She idolized him so much that she couldn't even bond with her mother until after he died. From New York to Nowhere: She moves from a posh neighborhood in Boston to a small town in Colorado to attend a doctor occupation there. At first she has a hard time adjusting to much less privileged life aside from facing the townspeople's backwards attitude towards her as a female doctor. Frontier Doctor: She becomes the first doctor Colorado Springs ever had. Until then, all they had was the local barber, Jake, whose ideas for aid could be,innefectual to put it mildly. Mama Bear: Although she initially struggles to become a mother figure to the orphaned Cooper kids, she eventually becomes firmly protective of them.

characters.name: Dr. Michaela "Mike" Quinn
characters.description: City Mouse: She grew up in a posh Boston neighborhood and has a hard time adjusting to the life in a frontier town. Daddy's Girl: She was beloved by her father, who wanted a son to follow his footsteps and so encouraged her to become a doctor like him. She idolized him so much that she couldn't even bond with her mother until after he died. From New York to Nowhere: She moves from a posh neighborhood in Boston to a small town in Colorado to attend a doctor occupation there. At first she has a hard time adjusting to much less privileged life aside from facing the townspeople's backwards attitude towards her as a female doctor. Frontier Doctor: She becomes the first doctor Colorado Springs ever had. Until then, all they had was the local barber, Jake, whose ideas for aid could be,innefectual to put it mildly. Mama Bear: Although she initially struggles to become a mother figure to the orphaned Cooper kids, she eventually becomes firmly protective of them.

characters.name: Byron Sully
characters.description: The enigmatic town loner. Sully was once married to Loren's daughter, Abigail, who died during childbirth along with the daughter she was carrying. Sully was heartbroken after the death and became somewhat of a hermit, living in the wilderness with the Cheyenne. He learned their ways and became skilled with a tomahawk. Due to his strong association with the Cheyenne, he acts as a liáson between the Native Americans and the American soldiers. Action Dad: To the Coopers after becoming their adopted father. Last-Name Basis: Everyone calls Sully by his last name - even his love interest (and later wife) and their children! His full name is Byron Sully, but he appears to have abandoned his given name around the time he deserted the army. Papa Wolf: He's very protective of the Cooper kids, both before and after marrying their adoptive mother.

characters.name: Matthew Cooper
characters.description: Matthew is the oldest son of Charlotte and Ethan Cooper, and the brother of Colleen and Brian. After his mother's death, he was reluctant to embrace Michaela as his mother or guardian. Matthew eventually grew to respect and love Michaela; however, they still have the occasional disagreement. He later becomes the town sheriff. Big Brother Instinct: He once threatened to kill some thug boys who tried to rob Colleen. Drowning My Sorrows: After his fiancée, Ingrid, dies from a rabies infection, he becomes an alcoholic. Pride: He is a very proud individual, who hates to accept help (or charity) from anyone. This becomes the source of many a conflict. Promoted to Parent: Before there was Sully, he was the oldest man in the family.

characters.name: Colleen Cooper
characters.description: The second child of Charlotte and Ethan Cooper. Formerly a boy-crazy romantic, she develops a passion for medicine through Michaela. Her interest and love for the medical field eventually leads to Michaela allowing her to help in the clinic. Academically inclined, and exceedingly proficient in mathematics, she plans to attend college in Denver to get her M.D. Alliterative Name:ColleenCooper, later becomesColleenCooperCook. Birds of a Feather: She and her love interest and eventual husband Andrew Cook are both children of doctors who seek to follow their parent's footsteps. Book Worm: Colleen loves to read, and initially acts as the town's librarian (when not helping Michaela at the clinic). Commuting on a Bus: She leaves the show for a while while attending the Colorado Seminary College.

characters.name: Brian Cooper
characters.description: The youngest of the three Cooper children. He was very young when his mother, Charlotte Cooper, died of a rattlesnake bite. He welcomed Michaela warmly, and was the first to refer to her as "Ma." Brian has a true love of animals, and has had two dogs, Pup, and Fifi. Brian has varied interests, including the ways of the Cheyenne, painting, baseball, and writing. He has a close relationship with Loren Bray, who would like Brian to work for him. Instead, he becomes a reporter for Dorothy's Gazette. Bratty Half-Pint: Being the youngest of the Cooper children, he occasionally succumbs to this. (Though rarely with any ill intent.) Friend to All Living Things: He loves all animals, and tries hard to accept and get along with everybody. Morality Pet: To Loren, who has a soft spot for him and even manages to control his temper when he's around.

characters.name: Horace Bing
characters.description: Driven to Suicide: He tried suicide by swallowing several pills when his marriage to Myra was over. He survives though. Endearingly Dorky: He's quite awkward, pays Hank just to be able to talk to Myra, and has jitters during his wedding night (not surprising since he's a virgin marrying a prostitute). He's also a pro at telegraphy, making him (more or less) the 19th century equivalent of an IT specialist. He's a nice man and Myra likes him, but other than that he's not seen as extra cute or attractive. Inciting Incident: His mistaking the lastain "Michaela" for an initial led the town to accept Dr. Quinn, unaware that she was a woman, which is was what kickstarted the events of the show. Western Union Man: He's the telegraph operator.

characters.name: Myra Bing
characters.description: Myra is introduced as a prostitute, working at Hank Lawson's saloon. She develops a romance with Horace Bing, later leaving her old life behind and marrying Horace. She later gives birth to their daughter Samantha. She eventually accepts a job from Preston to work as a bank teller. Florence Nightingale Effect: She started getting close to her eventual husband Horace while tending to him when he was struck with influenza. Hooker with a Heart of Gold: When there was an outbreak during the pilot, she was one of the few people who offered to help care for the sick people, even though Olive initially dismissed her for being a "whore". Later when an orphan girl considers accepting a job at Hank's saloon, Myra makes clear what Hank expects her to do. Myra keeps her own contract with Hank to keep him from exploiting the girl like he did Myra. Slut-Shaming: On the receiving end of this at times, once when Olive refused to let her help tend to the people during an epidemic before Dr. Mike intervened and later when her husband thinks she's thinking of going back to being a prostitute just because she read a book about a woman who commits adultery.

characters.name: Loren Bray
characters.description: The elderly owner and proprietor of the town's mercantile. Big Brother Instinct: According to his sister Olive, he used to protect her from the other boys when they were young. Grumpy Old Man: And how. He's irritable, sarcastic and mean-spirited but still has a good relationship with the other townspeople. Intergenerational Friendship: He's close to Brian, often acting like a grandfather to the kid. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: Loren is irascible, grumpy and often displays the typical bigotry of the time period. But he also cares about the other townspeople and is shown to have a soft spot for Brian as a surrogate grandson.

characters.name: Robert E.
characters.description: The blacksmith of Colorado Springs. Once a slave, his children were taken from him to face the same fate. He later marries Grace. Burly Blacksmith: He's the local blacksmith and has a fairly cool, no-nonsense attitude to boot. Dark and Troubled Past: He was once a slave, and killed his master while running away. His brother was killed in the process, as well. His feverish dreams also seem to point to him having previous children who were sold away from him and their mother. Don't You Dare Pity Me!: Declines Grace's initial attempts at visiting him after he's injured in a forge explosion, not wanting her to see him in such a state. Heteronormative Crusader: Is very distrusting of Walt Whitman after he learns that the author "prefers the company of men". He's surprised by Grace's accepting reaction to it, considering her religiosity, but having lived in New Orleans she knew that most of the prominent families had that one aunt or uncle that always seemed to prefer being more in the company of their own sex.

characters.name: Grace
characters.description: Originally from New Orleans, Grace was formerly Olive's housekeeper, and the two collaborated to open Grace's Café. When Olive passed away, Grace inherited her half of the café. She's later married to Robert E. The Alcoholic: She starts drinking after the death of her and Robert E.'s adopted son. Feminine Women Can Cook: Although she starts as one of Olive's cattle hands, she's also an excellent cook and eventually opens her own café in town after inheriting one of Olive's lands. Only One Name: If Grace had or adopted a surname, it wasn't told in the show. Outliving One's Offspring: Her and Robert E.'s adopted son Anthony dies from sickle cell anemia. The grief leads her to alcoholism and nearly causes them to split.

characters.name: Dorothy Jennings
characters.description: The sister of Maude Bray, sister-in-law of Loren Bray, and best friend of Dr. Mike. She is the editor of the Colorado Springs Gazette. Character Development: At first she didn't like or trust the Cheyenne, and refused to let them treat her son with their medicine. She comes to respect them and form a relationship with Cloud Dancing. Dark and Troubled Past: She came to Colorado Springs to escape her abusive husband. Mistaken for Pregnant: She notices what she thinks are signs of pregnancy, despite being separate from her husband for years (she did meet him once and is said to have done "more than just talking"), but it turns out that she's starting her menopause.

characters.name: Reverend Timothy Johnson
characters.description: The town Reverend, he doesn't always want to get involved in things. Was once a heavy gambler and card shark before being saved and becoming a Reverend. Chekhov's Skill: His past as a conman came in handy when the town was scammed by a pair of grifters. As he was in Denver with Dr. Mike on an errand, the town wired them and alerted them that the scammers were headed that way. Pretending to be a married couple, the Reverend pickpocketed the town's money back. Church of Saint Genericus: What denomination of Protestant Christianity he preaches is never brought up in the series. Dark and Troubled Past: It's revealed that he was once a criminal, living a life of crime and gambling, before finding his path in religion. Good Shepherd: He's a genuinely pious, caring minister who seeks to help the townspeople in God's path.

characters.name: Hank Lawson/Hans Lawsenstrom
characters.description: Co-owner of the Gold Nugget Saloon and Hotel. Hank also runs a brothel out of the Gold Nugget (and formerly ran one in town). An antagonist to most people in the town, Hank has a bad reputation. Bad People Abuse Animals: He once beats a horse of his when he can't get the poor animal to obey him. Disproportionate Retribution: Beats up Ethan Cooper after the latter displays bad poker etiquette in quitting the game after winning a big hand, thus denying him a chance to win his money back. Even Bad Men Love Their Mamas: Well, grandma. Hank is not the nicest man, but he adores his grandmother and when she comes to visit him, he tries to hide from her the fact that he owns a brothel, and pretends he has a respectable business and a family. Hidden Depths: He's shown to be highly observant, with a good understanding of human behavior. In Season 3, he correctly deduces that Mike's sister Marjorie is antagonistic and bitter because she's envious of Mike's engagement to Sully (due to her own failed romances), and that she has an STD (based on her symptoms). He alerts Mike to both."The fevers, those stomach aches that come and go. I seen it in my girls plenty of times... That husband left her a little more'n anybody suspects."It's also revealed that he was jealous and possessive of Myra because he was in love with her — and that he'd previously married another of his "girls" (who gave him an autistic son).

characters.name: Preston Lodge
characters.description: The owner of the Bank of Colorado, and the Colorado Springs Chateau and Health Resort. His role is often antagonistic, shifty, and somewhat showy due to his business-driven personality. Alas, Poor Villain: When his business fails at his last appearance, it's not hard to feel for him, especially since it was due to an honest mistake and he was looking forward to impressing his father, only to fail right in front of him. Opportunistic Bastard: He often takes the first opportunity to make a profit and doesn't care if others aren't pleased. Villainy-Free Villain: A lot of his business ideas are shady, but he's not exactly dishonest about them, just shifty and antagonistic.

characters.name: Jake Slicker
characters.description: Originally from Philadelphia, Jake is Colorado Springs' barber. He was once relied upon as the doctor as well (until Michaela came to town). He is later elected mayor of Colorado Springs, and becomes co-owner of the local hotel / saloon, the Gold Nugget. The Alcoholic: He still struggles with alcoholism, in part due to hisDark and Troubled Past. Dangerously Close Shave: Accidentally cuts a customer with his razor, which was not properly sanitized, giving the customer blood poisoning that they eventually die from. This kicks off his alcoholism getting worse due to the guilt. Dark and Troubled Past: His father abandoned the family when he was very young, leaving Jake to be raised by his highly abusive mother. Everyone Has Standards: Jake doesn't like children, and often chases them away from his barber shop with a broom. However, he would never hurt a child, having been abused as one himself.

characters.name: Cloud Dancing
characters.description: Heterosexual Life-Partners: With Sully. Maligned Mixed Marriage: Starts a relationship with Dorothy, against the townspeople and his own tribe's will.

characters.name: Maude Bray
characters.description: Loren Bray's wife and the sister of Dorothy. She dies in the pilot. Brooding Boy, Gentle Girl: The Gentle Girl to Loren's Brooding Boy. She's much more patient than him and tries to keep his temper under control. The Lost Lenore: To Loren after her death in the pilot. Outliving One's Offspring: She and Loren outlived their daughter Abigail, who died in childbirth along with her baby daughter. We Hardly Knew Ye: She dies in the pilot, and her death haunts both her husband and Dr. Mike due to it happening when she ran out of meddical supplies to properly tend for her.

characters.name: Elizabeth Quinn
characters.description: Michaela's mother. She was skeptical about her daughter's idea to pursue a job at a frontier town where people likely wouldn't accept and respect her. Bookends:The series started with the death of Michaela's father, and the final TV movie involves her mother Elizabeth's death. Fantasy-Forbidding Parent: She's the most cynical at her daughter's idea to pursue a job as doctor after her husband dies, since she knows the chances someone would hire a woman as a doctor are almost nonexistent. She later comes to accept Michaela's life choices after seeing what she accomplished in Colorado Springs. Outliving One's Offspring: Her daughter Marjorie dies from an epidemic that falls on Colorado Springs when she was staying there. So Proud of You: She eventually warms up to her daughter's ambitions and shows her pride by describing her as "single-handedly bringing modern medicine to Colorado Springs" during a party she attended with Michaela and Sully.

characters.name: Dr. Josef Quinn
characters.description: Michaela's deceased father. Hidden Depths: According to Michaela, he was an abolitionist and taught her "we're all equals", boosting his daughter's opposition to bigotry. Open-Minded Parent: He encouraged Michaela's becoming a doctor, partially because after having several daughters, she was the closest he ever had to a son. Parental Favoritism: He was closer to Michaela than any of his other daugthers, due to encouraging her to be like the son he always wanted. Plot-Triggering Death: His death meant the end of patients at his office where Michaela worked with him, leading her to pursue a job somewhere else and kickstarting her journey to Colorado Springs.
